content.problemStatement:
Now do the reverse, write a C program that converts a temperature in Fahrenheit to Celsius. The Fahrenheit temperature should be stored in a float variable fahrenheit, and the Celsius equivalent should be printed using a single printf statement.

We can use the same relation between the two scales:

`fahrenheit = (celsius * 9/5) + 32;
`Take the Fahrenheit temperature to be 98.3.
constraints: All input values have at most 2 digits after the decimal point
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a floating‑point number F, the temperature in Fahrenheit.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the temperature in Celsius, rounded to exactly 5 digits after the decimal point, on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Convert 98.3°F to Celsius using the formula, resulting in 36.83333°C.

solution.approach: For each Fahrenheit value we apply the inverse of the given relation: Celsius = (Fahrenheit - 32) * 5/9. The computation uses double precision to avoid overflow and rounding errors. The result is printed with exactly five digits after the decimal point using formatted output. The algorithm processes each test case independently, so the total time is linear in the number of test cases.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is handled in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space besides input and output buffers.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));String line=br.readLine();if(line==null||line.isEmpty())return;int T=Integer.parseInt(line.trim());StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();for(int i=0;i<T;i++){String s=br.readLine();while(s!=null&&s.isEmpty())s=br.readLine();double f=Double.parseDouble(s.trim());double c=(f-32.0)*5.0/9.0;sb.append(String.format(java.util.Locale.US,"%.5f",c));if(i<T-1)sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
